_warning: HAND-AUTHORED. A sync script that writes this file is a bug. This is the human override door for bounty progressions: a row here REPLACES the whole wiki-parsed history for that character, so if you touch a character you own all of their rows.
_what_the_machine_already_does: scripts/sync_wiki_characters.py parses the Char Box bounty field into {order, amount, as_of_chapter} and scripts/normalize.py merges it onto characters[].bounty_history. It resolves the wiki's named references ({{Qref|name=Enies Lobby Bounty}} is defined once and back-referenced after, so the chapter is indexed from the whole page) and the name=cNNN convention. That currently chapters every row for all ten Straw Hats, which is why the list below is empty: there is nothing left to repair by hand. The door exists for when there is.
_the_two_axes: order is STORY time (0 = the newest bounty). as_of_chapter is READER time (when that row was shown). They are NOT the same axis and neither derives from the other: Jinbe's 250,000,000 is revealed ch. 528 and his EARLIER 76,000,000 is revealed ch. 622, in a flashback. The gate is therefore 'among rows revealed by your chapter, take the lowest order' — never 'the latest chapter', which would hand a ch. 700 reader 76,000,000.
_the_drop_rule: normalize.py keeps only rows with a chapter. An amount with no as_of_chapter cannot be gated, and an ungated bounty is the exact spoiler this whole app exists to prevent — a databook or Vivre Card number would otherwise sit on a wanted card from chapter 1. Dropped rows are counted in meta.warnings, never silently. Today that costs five render-surface characters their bounty (Marco, King, Shanks's first, Ace's, Bepo's): they read 'no bounty posted yet', which is honest. Give one a chapter here and it lights up.
_how_to_add: {"slug": "portgas-d-ace", "rows": [{"amount": 550000000, "as_of_chapter": 552, "source_ref": "Hand-authored. Ace's bounty on the poster shown at ...", "canon_confidence": "canon", "verified": false}]}. slug is the canon character slug (data/canon.json characters[].slug), not the presence slug. Do not guess a chapter to make a number appear: a wrong chapter leaks a bounty early, which no downstream check can catch.
